name: Sy Johnson
abstract: Sivert Bertil Johnson Jr. (April 15, 1930 – July 26, 2022) was an American jazz composer, arranger, and pianist who worked with Charles Mingus in the 1960s and 1970s.

concerts.label: Tribute to Charles Mingus: Mingus Music

works.t: Don't Be Afraid, the Clown's Afraid Too
The Shoes of the Fisherman's Wife (Are Some Jive Ass Slippers)

about_quotes.speaker: Milt Hinton
about_quotes.evidence: Sy Johnson could tell you stories about him, you know. Because he called Sy Johnson and asked Sy to come out to help him fix his repertoire...
about_quotes.doc_title: Milt Hinton interviewed by Joe Williams, Majesty of the Seas, May 31, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: Probably Sy Johnson called me. He was kind of in charge of all that. ... it was Dave Berger and Sy Johnson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Roland Hanna
about_quotes.evidence: It wasn’t until later that he started to work with Sy Johnson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Roland Hanna interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 15, 2000
